title: lycopersicon esculentum subsp. humboldtii
intro: Lycopersicon esculentum subsp. humboldtii, also known as the Humboldt tomato, is a wild subspecies of the tomato plant that is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant's fruit has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Lycopersicon esculentum subsp. humboldtii include reducing inflammation and oxidative stress, as well as its potential to help manage blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. Lycopersicon esculentum subsp. humboldtii can be prepared as an herbal remedy through infusion, decoction, or tincture, although more research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and optimal uses.
